title: Red Hat Security Advisory: webkit2gtk3 security update
overview:
An update for webkit2gtk3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AppStream EUS (v.9.6)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: webkit2gtk3 security update

An update for webkit2gtk3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for webkit2gtk3 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-13502: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK and WPE WebKit. This vulnerability allows an out-of-bounds read and integer underflow, leading to a UIProcess crash (DoS) via a crafted payload to the GLib remote inspector server.

CVE-2025-13947: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. This vulnerability allows remote, user-assisted information disclosure that can reveal any file the user is permitted to read via abusing the file drag-and-drop mechanism where WebKitGTK does not verify that drag operations originate from outside the browser.

CVE-2025-43392: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. A malicious website may exfiltrate image data cross-origin due to improper handling of caches.

CVE-2025-43421: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause multiple issues in the JIT compiler and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43425: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause an unexpected process crash due to improper memory handling.

CVE-2025-43427: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause an unexpected process crash due to improper state management.

CVE-2025-43429: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause a buffer overflow due to improper bounds checking and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43430: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause an unexpected process crash due to improper state management.

CVE-2025-43431: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause memory corruption due to improper memory handling.

CVE-2025-43432: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause a use-after-free issue due to improper memory management and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43434: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause a use-after-free issue due to improper memory management and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43440: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause JIT issues due to improper checks and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43443: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause JIT issues due to improper checks and result in an unexpected process crash.

CVE-2025-43458: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause an unexpected process crash due to improper state management.

CVE-2025-66287: A flaw was found in WebKitGTK. Processing malicious web content can cause an unexpected process crash due to improper memory handling.
